I also request a 60-business day extension of the comment deadline, through June 29, 2026. The current deadline allows only 34 business days to comment. Moreover, the current deadline directly follows Easter and Passover holidays. This is an inadequate amount of time for workers, firs-responders, communities, families and other concerned members of the publi...

Children should never have to grow up in the shadow of a chemical disaster. Yet across the United States, over 117 million people live near industrial facilities that store and use highly hazardous chemicals. Strong safeguards under the Clean Air Act’s Risk Management Program help prevent catastrophic accidents and protect the health of children, workers, fi...

I strongly oppose the EPA’s proposal to take away these important safeguards that families need from chemical emergencies. Instead, I urge you to keep and implement the Safer Communities by Chemical Accident Prevention Rule under the Clean Air Act Risk Management Program and restore public access to chemical hazard information on EPA’s website. Over half of ...

I strongly oppose repealing numerous safety measures introduced in the new proposal. Stop work authority ensures that workers on the ground can serve as the first line of defense against a potential disaster by allowing them to cease production until all hazards have been identified and dealt with. If these provisions are reversed, companies could intimidate...

Residents everywhere, all across the country, have the right to safety from toxic chemicals. Companies should, at the barest minimum, be held to the utmost account when it comes to tracking air, water, and soil contaminants, as have been documented byproducts of waste facilities for decades. And at most, these companies should be required to seek and integra...
Employees are the best source of information about environmental risks in their workplace

I am alarmed that the federal government is proposing to roll back rules critical to protecting the American people from chemical disasters. That is shameful, not to mention scary! From everything we’re seeing it seems like Republicans don’t care at all about regular people, and that only corporations and the extremely wealthy matter. Otherwise they would ne...

As a citizen and taxpayer, I am against the Environmental Protection Agency’s proposal to take away vital safeguards from chemical emergencies. Instead, I urge you to keep and implement the Safer Communities by Chemical Accident Prevention Rule under the Clean Air Act Risk Management Program and restore public access to chemical hazard information on EPA’s w...

I oppose this proposed regulations change because it will weaken safety rules that protect workers, first responders and fence-line communities from catastrophes like fires and explosions at chemical facilities. More than half of Americans live near a chemical facility. One third of children attend school in a chemically vulnerable zone. Unfortunately, indus...

I'm writing in passionate opposition to the EPA proposal to reduce safeguards regarding chemical emergencies. We live in a dangerous world -- we MUST maintain procedures and funding for the safe response to emergencies. Anything else is short-sighted, irresponsible and reprehensible. Imagine if it were your loved ones affected by the next emergency.

Chemical safety isn’t a political yo-yo. Toxic chemical exposure can cause particular harm to children’s health and during pregnancy, and rolling back chemical safety standards won’t “make America healthy.” Basic protections against industrial chemical fires, explosions, and toxic leaks aren’t something that the Trump administration should keep bouncing back...
We need clean air to live. It is our birthright and necessary for our collective survival.
I oppose this proposal and demand that the EPA continue to allow third-party audits, stop-work orders in hazardous conditions, and public requests for impact reports from regional polluters.

It's horrifying how little this administration cares about our heroes that put their safety and welfare on the line to protect others. Some situations are especially dangerous and hazardous for a responder's health. They should absolutely be protected from the impact of chemical disasters. Corruption should not be allowed to rollback Biden Era protections.
I oppose any effort to revise the 2024 RMP rule.
